Both answers correctly identify density (not length) as the real axis, cite similar examples of long-form engineering-brand posts, and give an actionable cut-for-density prescription while keeping hard-won specifics. The difference is consistency: Answer A states 3,500 words is fine if every section earns it, but then concludes with an arbitrary target of 1,800-2,200 words, undercutting its own insight. Answer B sides with the author on the core principle (depth is the value), validates the reviewer's instinct about flab, and avoids setting an arbitrary target, instead offering a substance-based test for whether the length is earned. B is more internally consistent with the key point that length is the wrong axis.</parameter>
